g.text: Index Lookup vs Full Table Scan — users: 70,000 rows, 1,000 leaf pages, 4 KB/page
SELECT name, email FROM users WHERE id = 42
Performance Verdict — WHERE id = 42 on 70,000-row table
Full Table Scan | 1000 reads | ~100 ms | O(N) | selectivity 0.001% — still loses
Index Lookup | 4 reads | ~0.4 ms | O(log N) | 250× fewer reads, 250× faster
Crossover rule: prefer index when selectivity < page_count / (rows × RANDOM_IO_FACTOR)
Threshold ≈ 1000 / (70000 × 4.0) = 0.36% — index wins below this point
At 30% selectivity: 21,000 random table reads > 1,000 sequential reads → use scan
No usable index → cannot skip pages
id=42 found in Pg 0001 — scan cannot stop early
O(N) — scales linearly with row count
Phase 2: fetch full row by rowid (1 page)
id=42 < 23333 → descend left child ptr → Pg 204
40 ≤ id=42 ≤ 50 → descend right child ptr → Pg 207
id=42 found — extract rowid=42 for table lookup
Pg 001 | DATA | id=42 name=Alice email=alice@x.com age=34
rowid 42 lives in Pg 001 — full row retrieved
O(log N) — height grows as log base 341
